seo.title: Building SvelteKit form actions and progressive enhanceme...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for SvelteKit form actions and progressive enhancement with Vercel and Cloudflare Pages.

content.intro: This guide provides a step-by-step implementation approach for building a SvelteKit application with deployment flexibility, form handling, and AI integration. Focuses on production-ready patterns for performance-conscious developers.

steps.title: Initialize project with deployment targets
steps.explanation: Create new SvelteKit project and configure deployment targets in svelte.config.js. Use adapter-node for server deployments and adapter-vercel/adapter-cloudflare for edge computing.

code.content:
npm create svelte@latest my-app
npm install
npm install -D @sveltejs/adapter-node @sveltejs/adapter-vercel

pitfalls: Forgetting to install adapter dependencies for target platforms
Incorrectly configuring outDir for server deployments

steps.title: Configure environment variables
steps.explanation: Set up .env files for different deployment targets. Use $APP_ENV variable to conditionally load configurations for Vercel, Cloudflare, and Node.js.
pitfalls: Exposing sensitive variables in client-side builds

steps.title: Implement form actions with progressive enhancement
steps.explanation: Create form actions in +page.server.ts while maintaining client-side validation. Use form action URLs that work across different deployment targets.
pitfalls: Forgetting to handle form submissions in server-side actions
Not validating data on both client and server

steps.title: Integrate AI streaming response
steps.explanation: Implement server load functions that stream AI responses using ReadableStream. Use fetch() with 'response' mode to handle streaming data.
pitfalls: Not setting correct Content-Type headers for streaming
Missing error handling for failed AI requests

steps.title: Optimize for SEO and pre-rendering
steps.explanation: Configure prerendering for dynamic routes using src/routes/+layout.js. Use getStaticPaths() to generate static pages for search engines.
pitfalls: Forgetting to add dynamic routes to getStaticPaths

content.conclusion: This implementation pattern provides a foundation for SvelteKit applications that balance performance, deployment flexibility, and modern features. Validate configurations against target platforms and test streaming workflows thoroughly.
